headline: Oil Prices Dive As Trump Says Iran 'Excursion' May Soon Be Over; S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Oil prices, after jumping to nearly $120 a barrel, plunged below $90 on Monday amid news that Group of Seven countries may jointly release of petroleum reserves and on word from President Trump that the war may soon be over.  CBS News reporter Weijia Jiang said Trump told her in an interview that the U.S. is "very far" ahead of his initial estimate of a four-week conflict and that the war could be over "soon."  G-7 nations are set to meet Tuesday and might announce a release of reserves, buying some time for U.S. and Israeli combat operations to attempt to extinguish Iran's threat to Strait of Hormuz traffic and regional oil infrastructure.

headline: Oil Prices Back Below $100 Amid Iran 'Excursion,' Despite This Surprise
summary: Oil prices, after jumping to nearly $120 a barrel, came down amid news that G7 countries might announce a joint release of petroleum reserves on Monday morning.  The surprise was that the G7 stopped short of making such a move, saying only that they were prepared to act.  For now, markets largely align with President Donald Trump's comment to reporters on Saturday that the war was a "short excursion," suggesting that the oil price surge was temporary.

headline: 3 Reasons Exxon, Other Oil Stocks Are Rising as Iran War Drags On
summary: Exxon  Mobil and other 0il-and-gas company stocks gained early Monday amid escalating tensions in the Middle East and as investors positioned themselves for the possibility of extended supply disruptions.  Oil exploration and production companies  Exxon Chevron  and  Occidental Petroleum  were all up in premarket trading—by 0.7%, 0.7%, and 1.2%, respectively.  Natural gas companies had a similarly strong start to the week.

headline: DAL, UAL, CCL Tank, While LMT, RTX, OXY Gain Overnight As Oil Nears $120: Trump Dismisses Market Panic As 'Small Price' For Peace
